title: ARS (Architecture Risk Score) overlay by taxonomy class
question: Do architecture-driven variants (Class B) tend to live in more structurally fragile loci?

cross_locus_delta_lssim.note: delta_LSSIM from cross_locus_atlas_comparison.json used as fragility proxy
cross_locus_delta_lssim.hbb_excluded: HBB uses 95kb subTAD window, not comparable to 300kb windows
cross_locus_delta_lssim.reason: 5/8 loci have N_Q2b=0 (tied ranks); N=3 informative points insufficient

key_finding.description: BRCA1 has higher baseline fragility (6 severe zones, min SSIM=0.9236) but weaker Class B evidence (precision=3.8%). HBB has zero severe fragility zones but strongest Class B evidence (precision=100%). Pattern suggests baseline fragility does NOT predict Class B enrichment.
confounders: sample_size (N=2 loci with both fragility + Q2b data)

statistical_test.reason: Too many tied ranks (5/8 loci at Q2b=0) and N=2 informative fragility comparisons

conclusion: Insufficient data to determine whether baseline fragility predicts Class B enrichment. The two available loci suggest an inverse relationship, but tissue-match confounding and N=2 prevent causal inference.
recommendation: Generate fragility atlases for TERT, TP53, and ideally all 9 loci to enable proper cross-locus comparison. Use tissue-matched models where possible.
manuscript_impact: Not suitable for main text claim. Could be mentioned as exploratory observation in supplement with explicit N=2 caveat.
